kills: CONDITIONAL VACUITY: no p >= 2 phase gadget at k = 5 exists, is constructed, or is even shown abstractly possible. The proposal's own C4 predicts its proposed experiment returns negative on the entire lineage, and unlike L51 (which produced the abstract clamp in one move plus SAT verification) the proposal supplies no abstract alternator witness. So nothing unconditional is delivered and the headline theorem currently has no instantiation route except 'go find a new UDG', which is the same wall (W3 one rung up) the brief already names.

STALE NOVELTY / MISSED IN-REPO RESULT: C2's 'no one has computed the width-2 analog of the L55 monoid; that computation is itself a finite check worth doing' is false against the repo. experiments/combinatorial/w2_port_monoid.py (cache _cache/w2_port_monoid.json), spt_width2_stealth_classification.py, and w2_parallel_birth.py already did it. k5_edge: the ONLY stealth persistent edge-port relation is {e,s}, which contains identity loops, hence phase p = 1; so adjacent-pair interfaces provably cannot carry a stealth phase at k = 5, a fact the proposal needed and did not have (its stage 2 must exclude edge-port templates as sterile). k5_any: 6301 stealth-informative relations, and the FIRST maximal one is exactly the alternator (mono x rainbow union rainbow x mono); C2's named object already sits in repo artifacts at the relation level.

FALSE STEP IN C1's PROOF SKETCH: leak point (b) claims collision angles 'affect only a null/meager set of bundle parameters'. Wrong: internal coincidences of tau^n(P) with P are beta-INDEPENDENT (rotating the whole chain preserves them), so they are either always present or never. The clause is unfixable as stated; the correct repair is stronger and simpler: the pullback along S^1 x Z x V(P) -> R^2 is proper on the ABSTRACT chain regardless of coincidences or extra unit distances, because edges have unit length so never collapse and extra identifications only constrain colorings, and the phase property is universal over abstract-chain colorings. VERIFIER must rewrite this step; as submitted the proof contains a false assertion.

NO MOVEMENT ON chi(R^2) EVEN ON FULL SUCCESS: the only branch reaching chi >= 6 (C3 branch (a), rational angle with p not dividing L) is predicted empty by C3's own Niven/Kronecker analysis of rigid unit-distance angles. C2's 'strictly below the clamp' rhetoric conflates weaker hypothesis with weaker conclusion: the alternator needs less AND delivers less (chi_B/chi_BP, not chi). The program's ONE MISSING OBJECT is untouched; this is an Arch 4 pivot, not a W3 solution.

FACTUAL SLIP INHERITED AND REPEATED: 'all 12 lineage graphs, every one of ~2.29M pairs FREE' overstates L57, which records 11/12 graphs complete and the 874-vertex graph at ~96% at time of writing (experiments/LEARNINGS.md L57). C4's baseline must say 11/12 exhaustive + 874 partial unless the run has since closed.

EXPERIMENT DESIGN FLAWS: (1) Stage 1 restricts to 'Theorem-R cocircular templates', silently abandoning C4(i)'s universal quantifier; naive quadruple enumeration is ~10^10-10^11 per large graph and infeasible, but an exhaustive O(pairs) pre-filter exists (hash each non-adjacent pair by its 200-bit equality signature across sampled colorings; forced-XOR candidates = complementary signature classes, forced-correlation = identical classes). As written, C4 would not actually be decided. (2) Stage 2's generic 2-vertex separator inside a lineage graph does NOT instantiate C1's uniform-chain hypothesis (no congruence check |L1L2| = |R1R2|, no repeating gadget), so the escalation rule 'any p >= 2 hit is a chi_B >= 6 candidate engine' is invalid as specified. (3) Missing free consistency control: run the detector at k = 6 and k = 7; a stealth p >= 2 uniform-chain hit at k = 7 would contradict the Borel hex 7-coloring and must be treated as an implementation bug detector.

UNDERSELL WITH STAKES: with a generic-center Fubini argument (for a.e. translate of the rotation center, the restriction of a Lebesgue-measurable coloring to all finitely many interface circles is arc-length measurable, and the cocycle identity holds pointwise since properness is everywhere), C1's mechanism kills Lebesgue-MEASURABLE 5-colorings too: chi_m >= 6, which is the blocked Arch 2 headline target, and chi_B >= 6 then follows since chi_B >= chi_m. As stated ('Borel' + 'Baire') the theorem hides that a C2 gadget would moot the sparse conic solver blocker. Conversely note the trap that makes the fix necessary: a measurable coloring restricted to a FIXED null circle family can be non-measurable, so the Borel-only claim is what is safe without the Fubini step. Related: docs/research_atlas/arch4_set_theoretic_lineage.md is internally inconsistent on the ordering (line 71 says chi_B >= chi_m, correct; line 76 and 84 say chi <= chi_B <= chi_m, wrong); correct chain is chi <= chi_m <= chi_B.
prior_art_collisions: Kechris-Solecki-Todorcevic 1999: chi_B = 3 > 2 = chi for the irrational rotation graph; correctly cited as the k = 2 germ. The unit-chord-on-a-circle instance (radius r with 2r sin(theta/2) = 1) is HN folklore, mildly reducing the novelty of the bundle observation; the 5-color finite-UDG fiber generalization appears genuinely unstated in the literature.

Falconer 1981 chi_m >= 5; atlas (docs/research_atlas/README.md lines 56, 125) confirms chi_m >= 6 is OPEN in canonical R^2 (the cited chi_m >= 6 results are hyperbolic, DeCorte-Golubev, non-transferring) and chi_B has no published bound beyond 5. So the conditional conclusion is NOT subsumed. Note: CLAUDE.md's throwaway 'chi_m >= 6 in recent work' conflicts with the atlas's explicit misattribution audit; the atlas is authoritative.

REPO-INTERNAL (newer than the proposal evidently): experiments/combinatorial/w2_port_monoid.py + cache already classifies width-2 S_5-invariant relations; its k5_any maximal element no. 1 IS C2's alternator relation; its k5_edge result (only stealth persistent relation = {e,s}, loops, p = 1) proves edge-port phase sterility. w2_parallel_birth.py FACT A extends the L55 monoid obstruction to width-2 edge-port CHAINS (no pair forcing from mixed stealth products) without touching phases, consistent with and complementary to the proposal. spt_width2_stealth_classification.py states the matching 'width-2 stealth rigidity' conjecture.

L54's 'borel' retrodiction world failed the L-infinity detector on a norm-blind Borel/Steinhaus blade (experiments/LEARNINGS.md L54, known-kills list); this proposal is explicitly the repaired rotation-consuming version and does not re-propose the logged kill.

Polymath16 used irrationality of spindle angles for orbit-density constructions only, never as an ergodic lower-bound hypothesis; Marks determinacy (acyclic Cayley graphs) and Bernshteyn local-to-Borel (upper-bound direction) do not cover the cocycle-over-SO(2) lower-bound mechanism; Voronov map-type chi >= 7 is not implicated (no polygonal-region assumption anywhere).
result.surviving_kernel: The C1 reduction survives every structural attack I ran: if a finite UDG P has congruent non-adjacent interface pairs L and R = tau(L) (tau an infinite-order rotation, automatic when the pairs are congruent and not translates) and its extendable 5-color transfer digraph maps onto a directed p-cycle with p >= 2 (minimal case: the equality-alternator, XOR of pair-equalities), then either a finite subchain is non-5-colorable (chi >= 6 outright) or the irrational-winding cocycle kills every 5-coloring with Baire classes, and with the generic-center Fubini fix every Lebesgue-measurable one: chi_m >= 6, hence chi_B >= 6. Key structural virtues that survived: the realizability burden is only 'P exists as a UDG with two congruent non-adjacent pairs', with no cocircularity clamp equations, no closure, no contraction; angle irrationality flips from the W3 frustration to the load-bearing hypothesis; the k = 2 specialization is exactly KST; width-1 and edge-port width-2 cases are provably phase-sterile (L55 monoid + repo k5_edge result), so the alternator on non-adjacent pairs is the first genuinely unexcluded cell, independently corroborated as the unique clean maximal stealth-informative relation in the repo's own w2_port_monoid classification.
result.cheapest_decisive_test: Two cheap stages, in order. (1) Phase-detector validation: stage-0 controls (unit edge at k=2 gives p=2; triangle strip at k=3 gives p=3; 3-rail ladder p=1; J-I clamp chain at k=5 gives p=1, all confirmed by hand-checkable algebra) PLUS the missing k=7 hex-consistency control (any stealth p >= 2 uniform-chain hit at k=7 contradicts the Borel hex coloring and flags a bug). Minutes. (2) The actual decider for the kernel: attempt the L51-pattern ABSTRACT equality-alternator, i.e. one-move splits/products of K4-free 6-critical graphs (M^3(C_5) variants, Schrijver SG(14,5)) at <= 64 vertices, each candidate decided by exactly two cadical calls: 'eq(P1) AND eq(P2)' UNSAT and 'neq(P1) AND neq(P2)' UNSAT, with both pairs individually free as a stealth gate. Hours on the existing python-sat stack. If no abstract alternator exists in any constructible family (or a width-2 achievability theorem on the k5_any classification excludes graph-achievable subrelations of the alternator), C2 dies and the proposal has no instantiation route; if one exists, the program acquires its concrete new realizability target and the conditional theorem becomes load-bearing.
result.steelman: Reframed conditional theorem (corrected statement): let P be a finite unit-distance graph, omega(P) <= 3, with non-adjacent vertex pairs L and R = tau(L) for an infinite-order rotation tau about center O. Form the abstract bi-infinite chain by identifying R-of-copy-n with L-of-copy-(n+1); let C be the bi-extendable interface colorings at k = 5 and T the consecutive relation. Hypothesis: some psi: C -> Z/p, p >= 2, satisfies psi(b) = psi(a) + 1 on all of T (minimal case p = 2 via forced XOR of pair-equalities). Then: (no-lose) if C is empty, Konig gives a finite non-5-colorable subchain whose realized point set is a finite UDG, so chi(R^2) >= 6 in ZF+DC; (main) otherwise, pull any plane 5-coloring back along (beta, n, v) -> O + R_beta(tau^n v); the pullback is proper on the abstract chain regardless of coincidences or accidental unit distances (edges have unit length so never collapse; identifications only constrain), its interface states lie in C with consecutive states T-related, so f(beta) = psi(state at beta) satisfies f(beta + theta) = f(beta) + 1 everywhere; exp(2 pi i f / p) is an eigenfunction with eigenvalue outside {e^{i n theta}}, impossible for Baire-class f (meager/comeager rotation argument) and, for a.e. choice of center O by Fubini, for Lebesgue-measurable f. Conclusion: chi_BP(R^2) >= 6 AND chi_m(R^2) >= 6, hence chi_B(R^2) >= 6, from one realizable phase gadget, bypassing both the cocircularity clamp equations of Theorem R and the blocked Arch 2 sparse conic solver. Search discipline informed by the repo's own width-2 monoid computation: skip width-1 (L55) and edge-port width-2 interfaces (k5_edge: only {e,s}, p = 1); target non-adjacent pairs where the alternator is the unique clean maximal stealth-informative relation (k5_any); decide C4 on the lineage exhaustively via signature hashing rather than cocircular sampling; and pursue the abstract alternator by the L51 one-move pattern before spending anything on realizability.
